title: [Seq] Add a pass to convert an array seq.firreg to seq.firmem
body:
This PR introduces a new transformation pass `RegOfVecToMem` that converts register arrays following memory access patterns into `seq.firmem` operations.

This is required for the `circt-verilog` tool, to identify memories, such that other `circt` transformations/analysis can be run in the `seq` dialect on the `mlir` parsed from verilog.
 
The pass analyzes `seq.firreg` operations with array types and identifies memory access patterns consisting of:
- A register array representing memory storage
- `hw.array_get` operations for reads  
- `hw.array_inject` operations for writes
- `comb.mux` operations controlling read/write enables
- Optional output registers for read data

When a valid pattern is detected, the pass replaces the register array with a `seq.firmem` operation and corresponding read/write ports.

TODO: 
1. This pass cannot extract the read enable pattern yet.
2. Parallelize the implementation.

## Example Transformation

**Input:**
````mlir
hw.module @memory_example(in %clk: i1, in %addr: i13, in %data: i8, in %we: i1, out result: i8) {
  %clock = seq.to_clock %clk
  
  // Memory implemented as register array
  %mem_next = comb.mux %we, %mem_updated, %mem : !hw.array<8192xi8>
  %mem_updated = hw.array_inject %mem[%addr], %data : !hw.array<8192xi8>, i13
  %mem = seq.firreg %mem_next clock %clock : !hw.array<8192xi8>
  
  // Read operation
  %read_data = hw.array_get %mem[%addr] : !hw.array<8192xi8>, i13
  
  hw.output %read_data : i8
}
````

**Output:**
````mlir
hw.module @memory_example(in %clk: i1, in %addr: i13, in %data: i8, in %we: i1, out result: i8) {
  %clock = seq.to_clock %clk
  
  // Memory implemented as seq.firmem
  %mem = seq.firmem 0, 1, undefined, undefined : <8192 x 8, mask 1>
  %read_data = seq.firmem.read_port %mem[%addr], clock %clock enable %true
  seq.firmem.write_port %mem[%addr] = %data, clock %clock enable %we
  
  hw.output %read_data : i8
}
````
